goal: Inspect /app/a.out to confirm format, segment layout, and reference memory values so extract.js can match the expected output.
summary: Phase 1 confirmed: /app/a.out is an ELF64 PIE x86-64 binary, little-endian, with 4 PT_LOAD segments (vaddrs 0x0, 0x1000, 0x2000, 0x3db8) totaling ~2224 bytes memsz. e_phoff=64, e_phentsize=56, e_phnum=13. Reference solution likely adds 0x400000 base to every VirtAddr (matching example first key 4194304=0x400000), emits 4-byte LE uint32 words across each segment's memsz with zero-fill for BSS (8 bytes in segment 4).
stepSummaries:
1. Run readelf -l /app/a.out to enumerate PT_LOAD segments with vaddr/filesz/memsz. [verified]
  2. Run readelf -h /app/a.out to confirm class (32/64), endianness, and entry point. [verified]
  3. Sample known 4-byte words at virtual address 0x400000 (4194304) using xxd -e to confirm little-endian 32-bit word format matches example values. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: All 3 steps used real smoke verification on the actual binary (readelf, xxd, grep).
gateVerdicts.evidence: readelf -l, readelf -h, xxd -e commands all executed with real output.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to understand binary format and reference scheme; achieved.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Confirmed ELF64 PIE format, segments enumerated, reference base-offset scheme inferred from example.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred; all 3 analysis steps completed.
